CoachingSession.title: CoachingSession
CoachingSession.description: Represents a single interactive coaching session between a user and the AI coach. It tracks the lifecycle and key metadata of a session.

id.description: Unique identifier for the CoachingSession entity.

userId.description: Reference to the user who initiated this coaching session, provided by an external authentication system. (Relationship: User 1:N CoachingSession)

startTime.description: Timestamp when the coaching session officially started.

endTime.description: Optional timestamp when the coaching session officially ended. This field is null for active or uncompleted sessions.

status.description: Current status of the coaching session (e.g., 'started', 'active', 'ended', 'error').

createdAt.description: Timestamp when this session record was initially created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when this session record was last updated.

SessionTranscript.title: SessionTranscript
SessionTranscript.description: Stores the overall metadata for the transcript associated with a specific coaching session.

id.description: Unique identifier for the SessionTranscript entity.

coachingSessionId.description: Reference to the coaching session this transcript belongs to. (Relationship: CoachingSession 1:1 SessionTranscript)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when this transcript record was initially created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when this transcript record was last updated.

TranscriptEntry.title: TranscriptEntry
TranscriptEntry.description: Represents a single spoken utterance or turn within a coaching session transcript, detailing who spoke what and when.

id.description: Unique identifier for the TranscriptEntry entity.

sessionTranscriptId.description: Reference to the SessionTranscript this entry belongs to. (Relationship: SessionTranscript 1:N TranscriptEntry)

speaker.description: Indicates who spoke this entry (e.g., 'User', 'AI Coach').

text.description: The actual spoken text of the utterance.

timestampOffsetMs.description: Time offset in milliseconds from the start of the coaching session when this entry occurred.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when this transcript entry record was initially created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when this transcript entry record was last updated.

definition.description: Stores individual coaching sessions initiated by a user. The 'userId' field in the document matches the '{userId}' path wildcard, establishing direct ownership.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns the coaching session.

params.name: coachingSessionId
params.description: The unique identifier for the specific coaching session.

definition.description: Stores the metadata for a transcript belonging to a specific coaching session. Includes denormalized 'userId' and 'coachingSessionId' fields for authorization independence, allowing direct ownership checks without referencing parent documents.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns the parent coaching session and this transcript.

params.name: coachingSessionId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent coaching session.

params.name: sessionTranscriptId
params.description: The unique identifier for the specific session transcript.

definition.description: Stores individual entries (utterances) within a session transcript. Includes denormalized 'userId', 'coachingSessionId', and 'sessionTranscriptId' fields for complete authorization independence, enabling direct ownership checks without referencing parent documents.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns the parent coaching session, transcript, and this entry.

params.name: coachingSessionId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent coaching session.

params.name: sessionTranscriptId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent session transcript.

params.name: transcriptEntryId
params.description: The unique identifier for the specific transcript entry.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed following a strict hierarchical path-based ownership model, centralizing all user-specific data under `/users/{userId}`. This approach inherently supports private data and simplifies security rules by aligning document ownership directly with the authenticated user's ID (`request.auth.uid`).

Authorization Independence is achieved through strategic denormalization. For `SessionTranscript` documents, the `userId` (owner's ID) and `coachingSessionId` are copied from the parent `CoachingSession` document. Similarly, for `TranscriptEntry` documents, the `userId`, `coachingSessionId`, and `sessionTranscriptId` are denormalized. This ensures that every document in the hierarchy contains all necessary authorization context directly within its own fields, eliminating the need for `get()` calls in security rules to check parent documents. This is CRITICAL for robust security and efficient atomic operations.

This structure directly supports QAPs (Queries Are Permissions) by enabling secure `list` operations. For example, a user can query their `coachingSessions` or `sessionTranscripts` by simply filtering `userId == request.auth.uid`. Since the `userId` is present in every document (either directly in the path or denormalized into the document fields for subcollections), Firestore security rules can perform simple, direct checks like `allow list: if request.auth.uid == resource.data.userId;` or `allow list: if request.auth.uid == userId;` (where `userId` is the path wildcard). This allows Firestore's indexing to efficiently enforce security during queries, rather than relying on inefficient and insecure client-side filtering or rule-based `get()` operations, which would break query atomicity and debuggability. Structural Segregation is maintained as all documents within a given collection (`coachingSessions`, `sessionTranscripts`, `transcriptEntries`) share the same authorization posture (user-owned).
